metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC3's grep sweep to include `examples/` (and any other top-level agent-facing doc directories) so that `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` and similar files are covered, and update that file's 'remediate every valid finding' language to match the new judgment-based framing.
findings.finding: The AC's grep sweep scopes only to 'CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md', but `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136` contains 'remediate every valid finding' — mandatory framing equivalent to what is being removed from CLAUDE.md — and `examples/` is outside the sweep. Agents using that skill file will still receive the mandate being loosened, defeating the mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'No other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule (grep for "remediate" + "advisory" across CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `examples/` to the AC3 grep scope: 'grep -r "remediate" -- CLAUDE.md docs/ CONTRIBUTING.md examples/' and update SKILL.md:136 to mirror the judgment-based replacement framing (e.g., 'review findings and apply the ones that genuinely improve the plan; a BLOCK must still be remediated and re-reviewed').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the AC3 grep scope to include `examples/` (or explicitly decide whether `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136`'s 'remediate every valid finding' language should also be loosened to match the new judgment-based framing).
findings.finding: The AC grep scope ('CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md') excludes `examples/`, but `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136` contains 'remediate every valid finding' — mandatory-remediate language that directly echoes the CLAUDE.md mandate. The plan would pass its own AC3 grep without finding this echo.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC3: 'No other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule (grep for remediate + advisory across CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `examples/` to the AC3 grep: `grep -r 'remediate' examples/` and decide whether SKILL.md's mandatory framing should be updated in this same change or tracked as a follow-on.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 grep sweep to include `examples/claude-skills/` and decide whether `rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136` ('remediate every valid finding') and `rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` ('remediate findings before claiming') need parallel judgment-framing updates.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 grep sweep ('CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md') omits examples/claude-skills/, which contains at least two files with mandatory-advisory-remediation echoes: rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136 ('remediate every valid finding') and rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 ('remediate findings before claiming'). These are agent-facing skill guides that agents read directly and that mirror the CLAUDE.md mandate being loosened; if CLAUDE.md is relaxed but these skill files retain the mandatory framing, agents following the skill files would still loop on advisories.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `examples/claude-skills/` to the AC3 grep scope. In rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136, replace 'remediate every valid finding (fix the real ones; justify any you judge invalid)' with judgment-framed language consistent with the new CLAUDE.md text (e.g. 'review findings and apply the ones that genuinely improve the plan; BLOCKs must be remediated before re-running'). Apply the same adjustment to rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2, e.g. `grep -n 'BLOCK' CLAUDE.md | diff - <(git show HEAD:CLAUDE.md | grep -n 'BLOCK')` or a targeted `git diff` assertion confirming those lines are byte-for-byte identical to the pre-change state.
findings.finding: AC2 ('BLOCK-remediation and sign-every-round guidance are unchanged') has no proving command; an agent cannot produce evidence it was satisfied without a concrete check.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Verified by `git diff HEAD -- CLAUDE.md | grep -E "^[+-]" | grep -iE "(block|sign.*every|every.*round)"` producing no output (i.e. those lines are untouched).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that the observation plan (advisory-uptake rate and rounds-to-pass comparison via REVIEW_RESULT sidecars) is recorded on the epic.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes a deliverable — 'Record the observation plan on the epic: compare advisory-uptake rate and rounds-to-pass before/after via REVIEW_RESULT sidecars' — that has no corresponding acceptance criterion. No AC verifies that this tracking/observation artifact is produced.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] The epic records the observation plan: methodology for comparing advisory-uptake rate and rounds-to-pass before/after this change via REVIEW_RESULT sidecar norm_id survival.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out note: state the revert mechanism (e.g., restore CLAUDE.md lines 112-121 from git) and the threshold/signal that would trigger a revert.
findings.finding: The plan removes a behavioral mandate that agents currently follow (MUST-remediate-all-valid-advisories) without stating an explicit rollback or back-out path if organic advisory uptake degrades unacceptably after the change.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Rollback: if advisory-uptake rate falls below [threshold] after [N] rounds as measured by the observation plan, revert this change via `git revert <commit>` and re-evaluate the mandate text.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the grep sweep AC to cover the full repo (e.g., `git grep -r 'remediate' -- '*.md' '*.txt' '.github/' 'scripts/'`) rather than only the three named paths, so the 'No other repo doc' claim is verifiable.
findings.finding: The grep sweep in the AC explicitly scopes to 'CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md' but the criterion requires that NO other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule. Files outside that list (e.g., .github/ workflows, scripts/, README.md, top-level *.md files, or inline prompt templates) could echo the old mandate and remain unaddressed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'No other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule (grep for remediate + advisory across CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC line to: 'No other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule (run `git grep -ril "remediat" -- "*.md" ".github/" "scripts/"` and confirm only intentional references remain)."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or `docs/adr/` entry (or a committed `docs/advisory-remediation-policy.md`) capturing the field-data rationale, the new judgment-based framing, and the REVIEW_RESULT measurement methodology so the decision and its observability plan survive beyond the epic.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel process governance decision (shifting from mandatory to judgment-based advisory remediation, with a supporting observability framework) but does not create an ADR or persistent design doc capturing the rationale, field data, and the measurement methodology. The decision rationale lives only in the plan/epic, making it invisible to future maintainers who need to understand why the mandate was relaxed or how to interpret historical REVIEW_RESULT sidecars.
findings.location: What section / 'Record the observation plan on the epic'
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/NNN-loosen-advisory-remediation-mandate.md` with: (1) context — the field data (13 advisories/round, 95% churn, 4+-round tail), (2) decision — replace mandatory with judgment-based framing, (3) observability plan — norm_id survival metric definition and measurement cadence. Reference it from the CLAUDE.md edit with a one-line comment such as `<!-- see docs/adr/NNN for rationale -->`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and cite the 'convergence floors now default-ON (change 562)' claim: either point to the rebar.toml key, ADR, or Gerrit change that enables this floor, or remove/correct the assertion.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'convergence floors now default-ON (change 562)' as the stated rationale for the loosening, but no file in the repository matches 'convergence floors' or 'change 562'. This is a confident premise assertion that cannot be verified from the codebase.
findings.location: Why section: 'With the convergence floors now default-ON (change 562)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the parenthetical with a verifiable reference, e.g. the rebar.toml key name and the commit/ticket that enabled it, or remove 'change 562' and reference the observable config state instead.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
